# Claude Managed Agents on Blaxel (self-hosted sandbox cookbook)

> Anthropic hosts the CMA agent loop and environment work queue. Blaxel sandboxes run self-hosted tool execution. This repo uses an orchestrator sandbox for the webhook dispatcher and one worker sandbox per session. AI agents should read AGENTS.md first.

## Docs-only setup route

Read order for a cold agent: `AGENTS.md`, then `README.md`, then `.env.example`; use `GUIDE.md` for deeper narrative and `llms.txt` for a compact index. External links are reference material, not required for the main cookbook path after a human supplies accounts/access.

Human prerequisites: Blaxel workspace, `bl login`, Docker running locally, service-account `BL_API_KEY` for `BL_WORKSPACE`, Claude Managed Agents beta access, and `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`. `BL_API_KEY` lets SDK code and the orchestrator spawn workers; it does not replace CLI login for `bl push`.

Console-only gates: generate `ANTHROPIC_ENVIRONMENT_KEY` for the created Anthropic environment, and register the Blaxel preview webhook for `session.status_run_started` to receive `ANTHROPIC_WEBHOOK_SIGNING_KEY`.

## Core concepts

- CMA: Anthropic runs Claude, session state, event history, configured skills, and tool selection.
- Self-hosted execution: tool runtime, filesystem, processes, and network boundary live in the user's infrastructure.
- Blaxel orchestrator: FastAPI webhook on a public preview URL; verifies `session.status_run_started`, schedules background dispatch, readies scheduled and still-queued session sandboxes, claims queued work with the Anthropic SDK, and starts worker processes.
- Blaxel worker: `ant beta:worker run`; receives `ANTHROPIC_WORK_ID` and `ANTHROPIC_SESSION_ID`, runs tools in `/workspace`, heartbeats, posts results, and stops the claimed work.
- Worker image: self-hosted Docker image with `sandbox-api`, `ant`, and the language runtimes, package managers, database clients, and utilities from Anthropic's cloud sandbox reference. It smokes documented runtime floors, but it is not Anthropic's managed Ubuntu image.
- Optional Blaxel-native upgrades: Blaxel process records/logs prove which worker ran the tools; `example/demo_preview_resume.py` demonstrates public or token-protected private preview URLs plus standby/resume; `BLAXEL_WORKER_VOLUME_ENABLED=true` mounts a per-session Volume at `/workspace` when Volume quota is available.
- Optional public-preview upgrade: `BLAXEL_WORKER_PROXY_DESTINATIONS` + `BLAXEL_WORKER_PROXY_SECRET_VALUE` configure Blaxel Proxy secret injection for outbound worker requests. Keep Proxy opt-in, label it public preview and region-dependent, and run it in a proxy-supported region that matches the worker sandbox.

## Credentials

- `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`: local control-plane key. Creates environments, agents, sessions, and reads events. Never put it on the worker.
- `ANTHROPIC_ENVIRONMENT_ID`: self-hosted environment id created by `scripts/create_environment.py`; local shell, orchestrator, and worker process.
- `ANTHROPIC_ENVIRONMENT_KEY`: scoped, revocable environment key. The orchestrator uses it to claim work; the worker process uses it to serve one claimed session. Agent-run shell can read worker env vars.
- `ANTHROPIC_WEBHOOK_SIGNING_KEY`: `whsec_...` secret used by the orchestrator to verify Anthropic webhooks.
- `BL_API_KEY` and `BL_WORKSPACE`: Blaxel service-account auth for the orchestrator to spawn workers.
- `ANTHROPIC_AGENT_ID`: Managed Agent id created by `scripts/create_agent.py`; local shell only for example sessions.
- `ANTHROPIC_AGENT_MODEL`: optional local override used by `scripts/create_agent.py`; default is `claude-opus-4-8`.
- `BLAXEL_WORKER_PROXY_SECRET_VALUE`: optional public-preview Proxy secret, local shell + orchestrator only; never pass it to the worker process env.

## Canonical flow

| Step | Command/action | Env handoff | Side effect |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| 0 | Optional `python3 bootstrap.py --plan` / `python3 bootstrap.py` | bootstrap reads `.env` directly and appends generated ids | guided flow; stops at Console gates; uses default image publish names |
| 1 | `python3 scripts/preflight.py` | none | external read-only Anthropic access check |
| 2 | `python3 scripts/create_environment.py` | append printed `ANTHROPIC_ENVIRONMENT_ID` to `.env`, reload env | creates Anthropic environment |
| 3 | Generate environment key in Anthropic Console | append `ANTHROPIC_ENVIRONMENT_KEY`, reload env | creates scoped environment key |
| 4 | `(cd worker && bl push --workspace "$BL_WORKSPACE" --type sandbox)` | none | publishes `sandbox/cma-worker:latest`; use matching `--name` for custom `BLAXEL_WORKER_IMAGE` |
| 5 | `python3 scripts/create_agent.py` | append printed `ANTHROPIC_AGENT_ID`, reload env | creates Anthropic agent; set `ANTHROPIC_AGENT_MODEL` first only if needed |
| 6 | `python3 example/run_session.py --direct-dispatch` | none | creates real session + Blaxel worker sandbox |
| 7 | `(cd orchestrator && bl push --workspace "$BL_WORKSPACE" --type sandbox)` | none | publishes `sandbox/cma-orchestrator:latest`; use matching `--name` for custom `ORCHESTRATOR_IMAGE` |
| 8 | `python3 setup.py` | register printed webhook URL | creates or updates persistent orchestrator sandbox + preview URL |
| 9 | Register webhook, export `ANTHROPIC_WEBHOOK_SIGNING_KEY`, rerun `python3 setup.py` | append signing key, reload env | configures webhook verification |
| 10 | `python3 example/run_session.py` | none | creates real session; webhook dispatches work |

## Proof criteria

- `EXAMPLE: PASS` proves the Anthropic session completed.
- Worker attribution requires Blaxel proof: `bl get sandbox <cma-worker-sesn-...> process --workspace "$BL_WORKSPACE" -o json` plus `bl logs sandbox <cma-worker-sesn-...> <ant-run-...> --workspace "$BL_WORKSPACE" --period 1h`.
- Pass criteria: worker sandbox name matches the session id, process name starts `ant-run-`, command is `ant beta:worker run --workdir /workspace`, and logs show this session's `write` and `bash` tool dispatches.
- Required proof bundle: session id, claimed work id, worker sandbox, worker process, `EXAMPLE: PASS`, process proof showing `ant beta:worker run --workdir /workspace`, and log proof showing the same session/work through `write` and `bash` tool dispatches.
- Use one active claimant per self-hosted environment. If `workers_polling` or queued work appears, stop the webhook/orchestrator, stop other workers, or use a fresh Anthropic environment before treating the run as proof.

## Key files

- [README.md](README.md): shortest runnable path.
- [GUIDE.md](GUIDE.md): narrative source for an integration guide.
- [AGENTS.md](AGENTS.md): agent orientation.
- [scripts/](scripts): setup helpers; create scripts print exports and do not mutate `.env`.
- [orchestrator/app.py](orchestrator/app.py): webhook verify, fast dispatch scheduling, SDK work claiming, worker sandbox/process launch.
- [orchestrator/blaxel_features.py](orchestrator/blaxel_features.py): optional Volume config and public-preview Proxy config applied to both orchestrator and direct-dispatch worker creation.
- [worker/Dockerfile](worker/Dockerfile): agent runtime image.
- [worker/smoke.sh](worker/smoke.sh): command-surface and runtime-version smoke test for the worker image.
- [example/run_session.py](example/run_session.py): E2E example; `--direct-dispatch` claims exact work locally before webhook registration. After a webhook exists, Anthropic still sends `session.status_run_started`.
- [example/demo_preview_resume.py](example/demo_preview_resume.py): preview demo; use `--private-preview` for a token-protected Blaxel preview.
- [example/direct_dispatch.py](example/direct_dispatch.py): direct exact-work dispatcher used by examples; claims work from this machine, the worker still runs in a Blaxel sandbox.
- [tests/](tests): local tests.

## Invariants

- File tools use relative paths under `/workspace`; bash is still a shell inside the container.
- Tool results must be non-empty.
- `ant beta:worker run` uses `keep_alive: True` and a timeout cap.
- The orchestrator webhook server uses `keep_alive: True` so background dispatch can continue after a fast webhook response.
- `ant beta:worker run` owns the work heartbeat; the dispatcher collects near-simultaneous session starts, readies scheduled and still-queued session sandboxes before claiming, and then starts exact work promptly.
- `--max-idle` controls worker exit after the session goes idle with `stop_reason=end_turn`.
- `BLAXEL_WORKER_TTL` is max age from sandbox creation. It is a lifecycle backstop for abandoned workers, not idle deletion; standby/resume handles ordinary inactivity.
- Work process names are derived from `work_...` ids plus a unique suffix because Blaxel process records persist after completion and reclaimed work may need a fresh process.
- `EXAMPLE: PASS` proves transcript completion; Blaxel proof requires a matching `cma-worker-<session>` sandbox with the expected `ant-run-*` process/logs.
- Use one active work-claiming path per self-hosted environment during proof runs. Direct dispatchers, webhook orchestrators, environment-polling workers, and other cookbook workers all compete for the same Anthropic queue. `example/run_session.py` refuses to create a proof session while queue stats show queued work or active `workers_polling`; if a webhook is still registered, Anthropic can wake another dispatcher after the pre-check.
- One Anthropic environment per Blaxel workspace: the winning claimant creates the worker with its own Blaxel credentials, so `BL_WORKSPACE` does not pin where a shared environment's work lands. In webhook mode `example/run_session.py` verifies the worker sandbox and reports when another claimant ran it.
- Volume mode is per-session because a Volume can attach to one sandbox at a time. It requires Volume quota and `BL_REGION`, and mounts at `/workspace`.
- Proxy mode is public preview, region-dependent, and only for outbound application/API calls from the worker, not for replacing `ANTHROPIC_ENVIRONMENT_KEY`.

## Cleanup criteria

- Delete the orchestrator sandbox with `bl delete sandbox "${ORCHESTRATOR_NAME:-cma-orchestrator-app}" --workspace "$BL_WORKSPACE"` when done.
- Delete leftover `cma-worker-sesn-...` sandboxes only after sessions are inactive.
- If Volume mode was enabled, delete matching `cma-workspace-sesn-...` Volumes after workers are gone.
- Proxy config is attached to worker sandbox creation in this cookbook; deleting old test workers is the cleanup for changed Proxy routes, domain filters, and injected secrets. If the workspace exposes separate Proxy secret resources, delete throwaway `BLAXEL_WORKER_PROXY_SECRET_NAME` too.
- Published sandbox images are intentionally retained for reuse by default; teardown removes runtime resources, not registry artifacts.
- Disable/delete the Anthropic webhook registered to the Blaxel preview URL, revoke throwaway environment keys, and delete throwaway environments/agents.

## Links

- Anthropic self-hosted sandboxes: https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/managed-agents/self-hosted-sandboxes
- Anthropic cloud sandbox reference: https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/managed-agents/cloud-sandboxes-reference
- Anthropic Managed Agents overview: https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/managed-agents/overview
- Blaxel docs: https://docs.blaxel.ai
